title: Start and Test the WRD
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
Configuring for Web Deployment on iSeries
text: Start and Test the WRD You start and test the WRD from an administration page. After creating the and deploying the wrd.war file, use the IBM Web Administration for i page to start the WRD. On the IBM Web Administration for i page, select the Manage tab. There are four additional tabs under the top tabs. Select the Application Servers tab. In the Server: drop-down list (beneath the tabs), select the application server you have just created. If this application server is not running, click the green 'Play' button on the left to start the application server. Wait until the word Running appears to the left of the green button. In the left pane, under Applications , click Manage Installed Applications . On the right side, a list of installed applications is displayed. Among them is the WRD, listed by the name you gave it when you installed it. If it has status Running you can skip the next step, otherwise the WRD must be started. Select the WRD application and click Start . Note: If the Start button is dimmed, then the Enablement field tells you whether it has been started. If the field is enabled, it was started. Enabled applications start when the application server itself starts. If you left the TESTABLE servlet setting to true in Configure the WRD and Create a wrd.war File on iSeries , you can start another browser and enter the following URL: http:// iSeriesNetworkName : PortNumber / ContextRoot /wrd/run/WRD_Version Copy for example, http://myas400:10000/uniface/wrd/run/WRD_Version Copy PortNumber —port number you supplied in Create a New Application Server . ContextRoot —context root you supplied in Install the WRD in the Application Server . After a few seconds, you should see the Uniface Test Middleware response page. This indicates that the WRD and the application server are configured correctly. If you forgot what the context root or the port number is, you can click the Properties button and select the Context Root Port Mapping tab to find out. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
